questions.question:
A geographic area with an altitude of 400 metres has following characteristics:
Month  J  F  M  A  M  J  J  A  S  O  N  D
Average maximum temp. °C  31 31 31 31 30 30 29 28 29 30 31
Average minimum temp. °C  21 21 21 21 21 20 20 20 20 20 20
Rainfall (mm)              51 85 188 158 139 121 134 168 185 221 198 86
If this geographic area were to have a natural forest, which one of the following would it most likely be?
questions.subject: Geography
questions.explanation: The area’s mean monthly temperature stays above 18 °C and the annual rainfall is about 1,734 mm—conditions characteristic of the humid equatorial climate that supports tropical rain forests, not temperate or montane forests. ([gktoday.in](https://www.gktoday.in/analysis-of-upsc-civil-services-2010-gs-paper-part-1/))

questions.question: If a potato is placed on a pure paper plate which is white and unprinted and put in a microwave oven, the potato heats up but the paper plate does not. This is because

options.a: Potato is mainly made up of starch whereas paper is mainly made up of cellulose
options.b: Potato transmits microwaves whereas paper reflects microwaves
options.c: Potato contains water whereas paper does not contain water
options.d: Potato is a fresh organic material whereas paper is a dead organic material

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Microwaves heat substances through dielectric heating of water molecules. The potato contains substantial water, so it absorbs microwaves and heats up, whereas a dry paper plate lacks free water and therefore remains cool. ([gktoday.in](https://www.gktoday.in/analysis-of-upsc-civil-services-2010-gs-paper-part-1/))

questions.question:
With reference to the Constitution of India, consider the following:
1. Fundamental Rights
2. Fundamental Duties
3. Directive Principles of State Policy
Which of the above provisions of the Constitution of India is/are fulfilled by the National Social Assistance Programme launched by the Government of India?
questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.explanation: The National Social Assistance Programme provides social security assistance (Article 41) and is thus an implementation of the Directive Principles; it is not mandated by Fundamental Rights, nor does it relate to Fundamental Duties. ([gktoday.in](https://www.gktoday.in/analysis-of-upsc-civil-services-2010-gs-paper-part-1/))

questions.question:
A new type of El Nino called El Nino Modoki appeared in the news. In this context, consider the following statements:
1. Normal El Nino forms in the Central Pacific ocean whereas El Nino Modoki forms in Eastern Pacific ocean.
2. Normal El Nino results in diminished hurricanes in the Atlantic ocean but El Nino Modoki results in a greater number of hurricanes with greater frequency.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.explanation: A classical El Niño event warms the eastern Pacific, whereas El Niño Modoki features anomalous warming in the central Pacific; thus statement 1 is incorrect. Modoki events have been linked to enhanced Atlantic hurricane activity, making statement 2 correct. ([gktoday.in](https://www.gktoday.in/analysis-of-upsc-civil-services-2010-gs-paper-part-1/))

questions.question: Mon 863 is a variety of maize. It was in the news for the following reason

options.a: It is a genetically modified dwarf variety which is resistant to drought
options.b: It is a genetically modified variety which is pest resistant
options.c: It is a genetically modified variety with ten times higher protein content than regular maize crop
options.d: It is a genetically modified variety used exclusively for bio-fuel production

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: MON 863 is a Monsanto-developed transgenic maize line that expresses the Cry3Bb1 Bt-toxin, giving the plant resistance to corn-rootworm and other coleopteran pests; hence it was in the news as a pest-resistant GM variety. The other options describe traits (drought tolerance, high protein, bio-fuel use) that do not apply to MON 863.

questions.question: A cuboid has six sides of different colours. The red side is opposite to black. The blue side is adjacent to white. The brown side is adjacent to blue. The red side is face down. Which one of the following would be the opposite to brown?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: With red fixed on the bottom and black opposite it on top, the four side faces remain. Placing blue on any side forces white on an adjacent side (by the clue) and brown on another side sharing an edge with blue. Whatever the exact arrangement, brown and white end up on opposite side-faces (since the only face opposite brown cannot share an edge with blue). Therefore white is opposite brown; red or black cannot be opposite because those faces are already paired, and blue cannot be opposite because brown must be adjacent to it.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:
The satellite Oceansat-2 launched by India helps in
1. estimating the water vapour content in the atmosphere.
2. predicting the onset of monsoons.
3. monitoring the pollution of coastal waters.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Oceansat-2 carries the Radio Occultation Sounder (ROSA) that profiles atmospheric humidity (water-vapour), a Ku-band scatterometer whose wind-vector data are used by the IMD to forecast and time the monsoon onset, and the Ocean Colour Monitor that tracks chlorophyll, sediments and other indicators of coastal‐water pollution. Hence all three stated functions are correct.

questions.question: Genetically modified “golden rice” has been engineered to meet human nutritional requirements. Which one of the following statements best qualifies golden rice?

options.a: The grains have been fortified with genes to provide three times higher grain yield per acre than other high yielding varieties
options.b: Its grains contain pro-vitamin A which upon ingestion is converted to vitamin A in the human body
options.c: Its modified genes cause the synthesis of all the nine essential amino acids
options.d: Its modified genes cause the fortification of its grains with vitamin D

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Golden rice has been engineered with phytoene synthase and phytoene desaturase genes so that its endosperm synthesises β-carotene (pro-vitamin A); this carotenoid is converted to vitamin A after consumption, addressing vitamin-A deficiency. It does not boost yield, amino-acid profile or vitamin D content.

questions.question: In a tournament 14 teams play league matches. If each team plays against every other team once only then how many matches are played?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: In a single round-robin league each pair of teams meets once; the number of matches is therefore the number of combinations of 14 teams taken two at a time: C(14, 2) = 14 × 13 ⁄ 2 = 91. Hence 91 matches are played.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:
The Supreme Court of India tenders advice to the President of India on matters of law or fact
1. on its own initiative (on any matter of larger public interest).
2. if he seeks such an advice.
3. only if the matters relate to the Fundamental Rights of the citizens.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.explanation: Under Article 143 of the Constitution, the Supreme Court may render an advisory opinion only when the President specifically refers a question of law or fact to it; the Court cannot initiate such advice suo-motu, and the reference need not relate to Fundamental Rights alone. Hence statement 2 alone is correct, while 1 and 3 are wrong.

questions.question: Chlorination is a process used for water purification. The disinfecting action of chlorine is mainly due to

options.a: The formation of hydrochloric acid when chlorine is added to water
options.b: The formation of hypochlorous acid when chlorine is added to water
options.c: The formation of nascent oxygen when chlorine is added to water
options.d: The formation of hydrogen when chlorine is added to water

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: When chlorine dissolves in water it forms hypochlorous acid (HOCl); this weak acid penetrates microorganisms and, through oxidation, kills pathogenic cells. The action is not due to hydrochloric acid, nascent hydrogen, or hydrogen evolution, making option (b) correct.

questions.question: With reference to Lok Adalats, which of the following statements is correct ?

options.a: Lok Adalats have the jurisdiction to settle the matters at pre-litigative stage and not those matters pending before any court
options.b: Lok Adalats can deal with matters which are civil and not criminal in nature
options.c: Every Lok Adalat consists of either serving or retired judicial officers only and not any other person
options.d: None of the statements given above is correct

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.explanation: Lok Adalats can settle disputes both at the pre-litigation stage and those pending in courts, may take up civil as well as compoundable criminal cases, and are constituted by judicial officers along with social workers/other nominated persons. Therefore none of the three given statements is accurate.

questions.question:
Consider the following :
1. Bluetooth device
2. Cordless phone
3. Microwave oven
4. Wi-Fi device
Which of the above can operate between 2.4 and 2.5 GHz range of radio-frequency band ?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: The 2.4–2.5 GHz ISM band is used worldwide by Bluetooth, many cordless phones (2.4 GHz models), domestic microwave ovens (centered at 2.45 GHz), and Wi-Fi standards 802.11 b/g/n. Hence all four devices listed can operate in this frequency range.

questions.question:
Though coffee and tea both are cultivated on hill slopes, there is some difference between them regarding their cultivation. In this context, consider the following statements :
1. Coffee plant requires a hot and humid climate of tropical areas whereas tea can be cultivated in both tropical and subtropical areas.
2. Coffee is propagated by seeds but tea is propagated by stem cuttings only.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.subject: Geography
questions.explanation: Coffee thrives in hot, humid tropical climates, whereas tea is successfully grown in both tropical and subtropical high-rainfall regions such as the Himalayan foothills. While coffee is commonly raised from seed, tea can be propagated by both seeds and vegetative cuttings, so the word "only" makes statement 2 incorrect.

questions.question: In the context of the affairs of which of the following is the phrase “Special Safeguard Mechanisms” mentioned in the news frequently ?
questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: “Special Safeguard Mechanism” (SSM) is a provision being negotiated under the World Trade Organisation’s Agreement on Agriculture to let developing-country members temporarily raise tariffs to counter sudden import surges or price falls. Hence, the phrase is associated with the WTO, not UNEP, ASEAN-FTA or G-20. ([wto.org](https://www.wto.org/english/tratop_e/agric_e/negoti_mod1stdraft_e.htm?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Which of the following terms indicates a mechanism used by commercial banks for providing credit to the government ?
questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: Under the Statutory Liquidity Ratio, banks must hold a fixed percentage of their net demand and time liabilities in government-approved liquid assets (mainly Government securities). This mandatory investment channels bank funds to the Government and thus acts as a mechanism through which banks provide credit to the State. The other options refer to different monetary tools or concepts, not specifically to lending to the Government. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Statutory_liquidity_ratio?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
In order to comply with TRIPS Agreement, India enacted the Geographical Indications of Goods (Registration & Protection) Act, 1999. The difference/differences between a “Trade Mark” and a Geographical Indication is/are
1. A Trade Mark is an individual or a company’s right whereas a Geographical Indication is a community’s right.
2. A Trade Mark can be licensed whereas a Geographical Indication cannot be licensed.
3. A Trade Mark is assigned to the manufactured goods whereas the Geographical Indication is assigned to the agricultural goods/products and handicrafts only.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: 1) A trade mark identifies and is owned by a specific person or company, while a Geographical Indication (GI) denotes goods collectively produced in a particular area, so it is a community right. 2) Trade marks may be licensed or assigned, but Section 24 of India’s GI Act prohibits assignment, transmission or licensing of a GI; only ‘authorised users’ in the area may use it. 3) GIs are not confined to agricultural items; they also cover manufactured and industrial goods and handicrafts (e.g., Kanchipuram silk, Kolhapuri chappal). Thus statements 1 and 2 are correct, 3 is incorrect. ([interjusticelawfirm.com](https://interjusticelawfirm.com/en/transfer-of-intellectual-property-rights/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
The SEZ Act, 2005 which came into effect in February 2006 has certain objectives. In this context, consider the following :
1. Development of infrastructure facilities.
2. Promotion of investment from foreign sources.
3. Promotion of exports of services only.
Which of the above are the objectives of this Act ?
questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: The SEZ Act, 2005 aims at (i) developing world-class infrastructure, (ii) promoting investment—including from foreign sources—and (iii) boosting exports of both goods and services. It is not limited to services alone. Therefore objectives 1 and 2 are covered, while statement 3 is incorrect. ([commerce.gov.in](https://www.commerce.gov.in/about-us/subordinate-offices/offices-of-development-commissioners-of-special-economic-zones-sezs/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Which one of the following statements is an appropriate description of deflation ?

options.a: It is a sudden fall in the value of a currency against other currencies
options.b: It is a persistent recession in both the financial and real sectors of economy
options.c: It is a persistent fall in the general price level of goods and services
options.d: It is a fall in the rate of inflation over a period of time

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: Deflation is defined as a sustained (persistent) decrease in the general price level of goods and services, i.e., inflation falling below zero. Option (c) states this precisely. Option (a) refers to currency depreciation, (b) to a recession, and (d) to disinflation (a slower rise in prices). ([investopedia.com](https://www.investopedia.com/ask/answers/032415/what-difference-between-deflation-and-disinflation.asp?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
Consider the following statements :
1. Biodiversity hotspots are located only in tropical regions.
2. India has four biodiversity hotspots i.e., Eastern Himalayas, Western Himalayas, Western Ghats and Andaman and Nicobar Islands.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.explanation: Hotspots are defined by endemism + threat, and several lie in temperate zones such as California Floristic Province and the Mediterranean; hence statement 1 is wrong. India’s hotspots are Himalaya, Indo-Burma (includes N-E and Andaman–Nicobar) and Western Ghats–Sri Lanka—Western Himalaya is not listed separately and Andaman–Nicobar fall within Indo-Burma, so statement 2 is also wrong. Therefore neither statement is correct. ([gktoday.in](https://www.gktoday.in/analysis-of-upsc-civil-services-2010-gs-paper-part-1/))

questions.question: Widespread resistance of malarial parasite to drugs like chloroquine has prompted attempts to develop a malarial vaccine to combat malaria. Why is it difficult to develop an effective malaria vaccine ?

options.a: Malaria is caused by several species of Plasmodium
options.b: Man does not develop immunity to malaria during natural infection
options.c: Vaccines can be developed only against bacteria
options.d: Man is only an intermediate host and not the definitive host

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Unlike many viral or bacterial diseases, natural infection with Plasmodium does not generate long-lasting, sterilising immunity in humans; partial immunity develops only after repeated attacks and is species- and stage-specific. This weak naturally-acquired immunity makes it hard to design a vaccine that can elicit complete protection, which is why option (b) states the key hurdle. Options (a), (c) and (d) are either only partly relevant or incorrect. ([gktoday.in](https://www.gktoday.in/analysis-of-upsc-civil-services-2010-gs-paper-part-1/))

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:
1. The boundaries of a National Park are defined by legislation.
2. A Biosphere Reserve is declared to conserve a few specific species of flora and fauna.
3. In a Wildlife Sanctuary, limited biotic interference is permitted.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.explanation: Under the Wildlife (Protection) Act 1972 a National Park is finally notified and its boundary can be altered only by a resolution of the State Legislature, so statement 1 is correct. Biosphere Reserves aim to conserve entire ecosystems with core–buffer–transition zones, not merely a few species, so statement 2 is incorrect. Wildlife Sanctuaries do permit regulated grazing, firewood collection etc.; hence statement 3 is correct. Thus statements 1 and 3 only are right. ([edurev.in](https://edurev.in/t/203219/Geography-of-India-Solved-Questions--2007-2010-?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: A pesticide which is a chlorinated hydrocarbon is sprayed on a food crop. The food chain is: Food crop – Rat – Snake – Hawk. In this food chain, the highest concentration of the pesticide would accumulate in which one of the following?
questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.explanation: Chlorinated hydrocarbons such as DDT are persistent and fat-soluble; their concentration rises at each trophic level (biomagnification). In the chain food-crop → rat → snake → hawk, the hawk is the top predator and therefore accumulates the highest residue. ([gktoday.in](https://www.gktoday.in/analysis-of-upsc-civil-services-2010-gs-paper-part-ii/))

questions.question:
With reference to soil conservation, consider the following practices:
1. Crop rotation
2. Sand fences
3. Terracing
4. Wind breaks
Which of the above are considered appropriate methods for soil conservation in India?
questions.subject: Geography
questions.explanation: All four listed practices are recognised in India: crop rotation maintains soil structure and fertility; sand fences (or brush-wood fencing) and wind-breaks reduce wind erosion in deserts/coasts; terracing checks runoff on hill slopes. Hence every method (1-4) is appropriate. ([gktoday.in](https://www.gktoday.in/analysis-of-upsc-civil-services-2010-gs-paper-part-ii/))

questions.question:
With reference to the Non-banking Financial Companies (NBFCs) in India, consider the following statements:
1. They cannot engage in the acquisition of securities issued by the government.
2. They cannot accept demand deposits like Savings Account.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: NBFCs are permitted to conduct business that includes acquisition of shares, stocks, bonds and other securities—including Government securities—so Statement 1 is incorrect. However, the RBI explicitly bars NBFCs from accepting demand deposits such as savings or current-account money, hence Statement 2 is correct. Therefore only Statement 2 is true. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NBFC_and_MFI_in_India))

questions.question: Which one of the following was NOT stipulated in the Fiscal Responsibility and Budget Management Act, 2003?

options.a: Elimination of revenue deficit by the end of the fiscal year 2007-08
options.b: Non-borrowing by the Central Government from Reserve Bank of India except under certain circumstances
options.c: Elimination of primary deficit by the end of the fiscal year 2008-09
options.d: Fixing government guarantees in any financial year as a percentage of GDP

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: The FRBM Act mandated elimination of the revenue deficit by 31 March 2008, prohibited the Centre from borrowing from the RBI except under special circumstances, and capped fresh sovereign guarantees at 0.5 % of GDP in any financial year. It did NOT call for elimination of the primary deficit; that term does not appear among the statutory targets. Hence option (c) is the provision that was NOT stipulated. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fiscal_Responsibility_and_Budget_Management_Act%2C_2003?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Two trains leave New Delhi at the same time. One travels north at 60 kmph and the other travels south at 40 kmph. After how many hours will the trains be 150 km apart?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: When two objects move in opposite directions, relative speed is the sum of their speeds: 60 km/h + 40 km/h = 100 km/h. To be 150 km apart they need 150 ÷ 100 = 1.5 hours, i.e., 3⁄2 hours; so option (a) is correct.

questions.question: A question paper had ten questions. Each question could only be answered as True (T) or False (F). Each candidate answered all the questions. Yet, no two candidates wrote the answers in an identical sequence. How many different sequences of answers are possible?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Each of the 10 True/False questions can be answered in 2 ways, and choices are independent. Total distinct answer sequences = 2¹⁰ = 1024. Therefore option (d) is correct.

questions.question: In the parlance of financial investments, the term ‘bear’ denotes

options.a: An investor who feels that the price of a particular security is going to fall
options.b: An investor who expects the price of particular shares to rise
options.c: A shareholder or a bondholder who has an interest in a company, financial or otherwise
options.d: Any lender whether by making a loan or buying a bond

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: In market jargon a ‘bear’ (or bearish investor) is one who expects the price of a security or the market to decline and positions accordingly, e.g., by short-selling; hence option (a) is correct, while option (b) describes a ‘bull’. ([investopedia.com](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/b/bull-position.asp?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: A great deal of Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) to India comes from Mauritius than from many major and mature economies like UK and France. Why?

options.a: India has preference for certain countries as regards receiving FDI
options.b: India has double taxation avoidance agreement with Mauritius
options.c: Most citizens of Mauritius have ethnic identity with India and so they feel secure to invest in India
options.d: Impending dangers of global climatic change prompt Mauritius to make huge investments in India

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: Investors route funds via Mauritius because the India–Mauritius Double Taxation Avoidance Agreement (DTAA) exempts capital-gains tax in India for Mauritian-resident companies; this fiscal advantage makes Mauritius the largest conduit of FDI. There is no official country-preference, ethnic link or climate motive involved. ([gktoday.in](https://www.gktoday.in/analysis-of-upsc-civil-services-2010-gs-paper-part-ii/))

questions.question: Six books A, B, C, D, E and F are placed side by side. B, C and E have blue cover and the other books have red cover. Only D and F are new books and the rest are old. A, C and D are law reports and others are Gazetters. What book is a new law report with a red colour?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Only book D is simultaneously (i) new, (ii) a law report, and (iii) red-covered: the ‘new’ condition limits choices to D or F; among these, only D is a law report and it has a red cover (B C E are blue). Hence option D. ([gktoday.in](https://www.gktoday.in/analysis-of-upsc-civil-services-2010-gs-paper-part-ii/))

questions.question:
Following are the characteristics of an area in India:
1. Hot and humid climate
2. Annual rainfall 200 cm
3. Hill slopes up to an altitude of 1100 metres
4. Annual range of temperature 15 °C to 30 °C
Which one among the following crops are you most likely to find in the area described above?
questions.subject: Geography
questions.explanation: The described hot-humid hills (15-30 °C, >200 cm rain up to 1100 m) match the ecology of black pepper cultivation in the Western Ghats and parts of the Northeast. Cotton, mustard and Virginia tobacco require much drier or cooler regimes, so pepper fits best. ([gktoday.in](https://www.gktoday.in/analysis-of-upsc-civil-services-2010-gs-paper-part-ii/))

questions.question: Running at a speed of 60 km per hour, a train passed through a 1.5 km long tunnel in two minutes. What is the length of the train?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Speed 60 km h⁻¹ = 50⁄3 m s⁻¹. In 2 min (120 s) distance covered = 50⁄3 × 120 = 2000 m. Distance = tunnel length (1500 m) + train length (L). Therefore L = 2000 – 1500 = 500 m. ([testbook.com](https://testbook.com/question-answer/running-at-a-speed-of-60-km-per-hour-a-train-pass--607ffaa2df0244966307101c?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
India-based Neutrino Observatory is included by the Planning Commission as a mega science project under the 11th Five-Year Plan. In this context, consider the following statements:
1. Neutrinos are chargeless elementary particles that travel close to the speed of light.
2. Neutrinos are created in nuclear reactions of beta decay.
3. Neutrinos have a negligible, but non-zero mass.
4. Trillions of Neutrinos pass through human body every second.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Neutrinos are neutral leptons that move near light speed, are produced in β-decay reactions, possess a tiny yet non-zero mass (established via oscillation experiments), and solar/cosmic sources create a flux of trillions through each human per second. Hence all four statements are correct. ([gktoday.in](https://www.gktoday.in/analysis-of-upsc-civil-services-2010-gs-paper-part-ii/))

questions.question: The “Instrument of Instructions” contained in the Government of India Act 1935 have been incorporated in the Constitution of India in the year 1950 as

options.c: Extent of executive power of State
options.d: Conduct of business of the Government of India

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.explanation: The Government of India Act, 1935 included “Instruments of Instructions” to guide the executive; these were carried over into the Constitution of India as the Directive Principles of State Policy in Part IV (Articles 36-51), not as Fundamental Rights or other provisions. ([sarthaks.com](https://www.sarthaks.com/2715749/instrument-instructions-contained-government-india-incorporated-constitution-india?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Hydrogen fuel-cell vehicles produce one of the following as “exhaust”.
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: In a proton-exchange-membrane fuel-cell vehicle, hydrogen reacts with oxygen to generate electricity and the only by-product is water vapour (H₂O), which exits through the exhaust; no ammonia, methane, or hydrogen peroxide is produced. ([energy.gov](https://www.energy.gov/eere/fuelcells/water-emissions-fuel-cell-vehicles?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Recently, LASIK (Laser Assisted In-Situ Keratomileusis) procedure is being made popular for vision correction. Which one of the following statements in this context is not correct?

options.a: LASIK procedure is used to correct refractive errors of the eye
options.b: It is a procedure that permanently changes the shapes of the cornea
options.c: It reduces a person’s dependence on glasses or contact lenses
options.d: It is a procedure that can be done on the person of any age

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: LASIK permanently reshapes the cornea to correct refractive errors and usually reduces dependence on spectacles, but it is NOT performed on persons of any age: FDA guidelines set 18 years as the minimum because the refractive power must be stable; hence option (d) is incorrect while the other three statements are correct. ([lasik.com](https://www.lasik.com/age-requirements-for-lasik-eye-surgery/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
Consider the following:
1. Oxides of Hydrogen
2. Oxides of Nitrogen
3. Oxides of Sulphur
Which of the above causes/cause acid rain?
questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.explanation: Acid rain forms when emissions of sulfur dioxide (SO₂) and nitrogen oxides (NOₓ) react with atmospheric moisture to produce sulfuric and nitric acids; oxides of hydrogen (water) do not contribute. Therefore only oxides of nitrogen and sulfur (2 and 3) are responsible. ([britannica.com](https://www.britannica.com/science/What-Causes-Acid-Rain?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Six persons M, N, O, P, Q and R are sitting in two rows, three in each. Q is not at the end of any row. P is second to the left of R. O is the neighbour of Q and is sitting diagonally opposite to P. N is the neighbour of R. On the basis of above information, who is facing N?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Drawing the two facing rows (three seats each) satisfying all clues—P two seats to R’s left (so P and R occupy opposite ends), O next to Q and diagonally opposite P, Q in a middle seat, and N beside R—gives the layout: Row 1 (O Q M) facing Row 2 (R P N). The person directly facing N is Q. ([testbook.com](https://testbook.com/question-answer/six-persons-m-n-o-p-q-and-r-are-sitting-in-two--60801d06907e7564c1c27600?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: A person X has four notes of Rupee 1, 2, 5 and 10 denomination. The number of different sums of money she can form from them is
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Each of the four distinct notes (₹1, 2, 5, 10) can be either taken or not taken, giving 2⁴ = 16 subsets; excluding the empty subset (no money) leaves 15 distinct sums. Hence option (b) is correct. ([rishiupsc.com](https://rishiupsc.com/upsc-prelims-question-paper-2010-with-answers/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
Consider the following countries:
1. Brazil
2. Mexico
3. South Africa
According to UNCTAD, which of the above is/are categorized as “Emerging Economies”?
questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: UNCTAD’s ‘emerging economies’ (also called Big Emerging Markets) list includes Brazil, Mexico and South Africa; therefore all three countries in the question fall under the category, making option (d) correct. ([studocu.com](https://www.studocu.com/in/document/karnataka-state-law-university/llb-3-years/prelims-general-studies-2010-answer-key/27145276?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Which one of the following is not related to United Nations?

options.c: International Centre for Settlement of Investment Disputes

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: MIGA, IFC and ICSID are specialised agencies/affiliates within the World Bank Group, which itself is part of the UN system. The Bank for International Settlements is an independent institution for central-bank cooperation and has no UN affiliation, so option (d) is the one not related to the United Nations. ([bissoy.com](https://www.bissoy.com/mcq/1040348?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
In the context of Indian economy, consider the following pairs:
                Term                     –  Most appropriate description
1. Melt down          –  Fall in stock prices
2. Recession            –  Fall in growth rate
3. Slow down        –  Fall in GDP
Which of the pairs given above is/are correctly matched?
questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: ‘Meltdown’ is popularly used for a sharp crash in stock or asset prices, so pair 1 is correct. A ‘recession’ is defined by an absolute decline in GDP (negative growth for two consecutive quarters), not merely a lower growth rate, and a ‘slowdown’ denotes only a deceleration of positive growth, not a fall in GDP; thus pairs 2 and 3 are incorrect. Hence only pair 1 is correctly matched. ([testbook.com](https://testbook.com/question-answer/in-the-context-of-the-indian-economy-consider-the--60802212089e88d80ea35e6b?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
With reference to BRIC countries, consider the following statements:
1. At present, China’s GDP is more than the combined GDP of all the three other countries.
2. China’s population is more than the combined population of any two other countries.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: In 2009-10 China’s nominal GDP (~US $5 trn) exceeded the combined GDP of Brazil, Russia and India (~US $4 trn), so statement 1 is true. China’s population (~1.34 bn) was NOT larger than the combined population of India plus either Brazil or Russia (e.g., India + Brazil ≈ 1.37 bn); therefore statement 2 is false. Hence only statement 1 is correct, making option (a) the right choice. ([testbook.com](https://testbook.com/question-answer/with-reference-to-bric-countries-consider-the-fol--60802321420a3434062b4ae3?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Which of the following is/are treated as artificial currency?
questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: Special Drawing Rights (SDRs) are reserve assets created by the IMF and are often described as an international ‘artificial currency’. ADRs and GDRs are depositary receipts representing equity and are not currencies. Hence only SDR qualifies. ([imf.org](https://www.imf.org/en/topics/special-drawing-right?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Stiglitz Commission established by the President of the United Nations General Assembly was in the international news. The commission was supposed to deal with

options.a: The challenges posed by the impending global climate change and prepare a road map
options.b: The workings of the global financial systems and to explore ways and means to secure a more sustainable global order
options.c: Global terrorism and prepare a global action plan for the mitigation of terrorism
options.d: Expansion of the United Nations Security Council in the present global scenario

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.explanation: The Commission of Experts chaired by Nobel laureate Joseph Stiglitz was convened by the President of the UN General Assembly to review the workings of the global financial system and recommend steps for a more sustainable and just economic order. Therefore option (b) is correct; the commission was unrelated to climate change, terrorism or UNSC reform. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Commission_of_Experts_on_Reforms_of_the_International_Monetary_and_Financial_System?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
With reference to the mineral resources of India, consider the following pairs:
Mineral — 90% Natural sources in
1. Copper  —  Jharkhand
2. Nickel   —  Orissa
3. Tungsten —  Kerala
Which of the pairs given above is/are correctly matched?
questions.subject: Geography
questions.explanation: About 92 % of India’s nickel resources lie in Odisha (Sukinda and adjoining belts), so pair 2 is correct. Only ~19 % of copper ore reserves are in Jharkhand while around 50 % are in Rajasthan, making pair 1 wrong. India’s major tungsten resources are in Karnataka (≈42 %) and Rajasthan (≈27 %); Kerala has negligible reserves, so pair 3 is wrong. Thus only pair 2 matches. ([odishaminerals.gov.in](https://odishaminerals.gov.in/IndustryWindow/GrowthPotential?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: As regards the use of international food safety standards as reference point for the dispute settlements, which one of the following does WTO collaborate with?
questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: Under the WTO’s Agreement on Sanitary and Phytosanitary Measures, the body collaborates with the FAO/WHO Codex Alimentarius Commission for international food-safety standards used in dispute settlement. The other organizations listed are not specified in the SPS Agreement for this purpose. ([wto.org](https://www.wto.org/english/thewto_e/coher_e/wto_codex_e.htm?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: An objective of the National Food Security Mission is to increase the production of certain crops through area expansion and productivity enhancement in a sustainable manner in the identified districts of the country. What are those crops?

options.c: Rice, wheat, pulses and oilseeds only
options.d: Rice, wheat, pulses, oilseeds and vegetables

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: NFSM, launched in 2007-08, originally targeted additional production of rice, wheat and pulses through area expansion and productivity improvement; oilseeds and vegetables were not part of its initial crop basket. Hence option (b) is correct. ([pib.gov.in](https://www.pib.gov.in/PressReleaseIframePage.aspx?PRID=1698199&utm_source=openai))

questions.question: In India, the interest rate on savings accounts in all the nationalized commercial banks is fixed by
questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: Till October 2011 the interest rate payable on savings-bank deposits was administered by the Reserve Bank of India, not by the Finance Ministry, Finance Commission or the Indian Banks’ Association. Because RBI is not among the options, “None of the above” is correct. Options (a), (b) and (c) are incorrect because neither the Ministry, nor the Finance Commission, nor the IBA had statutory authority to fix these rates. ([upscgspedia.com](https://upscgspedia.com/upsc-prelims-economy-pyqs-money-banking/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: With reference to Simon Commission’s recommendations, which one of the following statements is correct?

options.a: It recommended the replacement of diarchy with responsible government in the provinces
options.b: It proposed the setting up of inter-provincial council under the Home Department
options.c: It suggested the abolition of bicameral legislature at the Centre
options.d: It recommended the creation of Indian Police Service, with a provision for increased pay and allowances for British recruits as compared to Indian recruits

questions.subject: Modern History
questions.explanation: The Simon Commission Report (1930) recommended that diarchy in the provinces be abolished and replaced by full-fledged responsible government with ministers answerable to provincial legislatures. It did not emphasise an inter-provincial council, abolition of bicameralism, or differential pay for an Indian Police Service, so options (b), (c) and (d) are wrong. ([sarthaks.com](https://www.sarthaks.com/2716549/with-reference-simon-commissions-recommendations-which-following-statements-correct?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Four resolutions were passed at the famous Calcutta session of Indian National Congress in 1906. The question of either retention OR of rejection of these four resolutions became the cause of a split in Congress at the next Congress session held in Surat in 1907. Which one of the following was not one of those resolutions?
questions.subject: Modern History
questions.explanation: At Calcutta (1906) Congress adopted four militant resolutions—Swaraj (self-government), Swadeshi, Boycott, and National Education. The demand for immediate annulment of the partition of Bengal, though central to the movement, was not one of the four resolutions that later provoked the Surat split; hence option (a) is not included and is the correct choice. Options (b), (c) and (d) were explicitly part of the four resolutions. ([prepp.in](https://prepp.in/question/four-resolutions-were-passed-at-the-famous-calcutt-6453b5753c1bc691f01fa330?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Two numbers X and Y are respectively 20% and 28% less than a third number Z. By what percentage is the number Y less than the number X?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Let Z = 100. Then X = 100 − 20 = 80 and Y = 100 − 28 = 72. The shortfall of Y from X is 80 − 72 = 8. Expressed as a percentage of X: (8⁄80) × 100 = 10 %. Hence Y is 10 % less than X; option (c) is correct. Other options mis-compute the percentage difference.

questions.question: After Quit India Movement, C. Rajagopalachari issued a pamphlet entitled "The Way Out". Which one of the following was a proposal in this pamphlet?

options.a: The establishment of a “War Advisory Council” composed of representatives of British India and the Indian States
options.b: Reconstitution of the Central Executive Council in such a way that all its members, except the Governor General and the Commander-in-Chief should be Indian leaders
options.c: Fresh elections to the Central and Provincial Legislatures to be held at the end of 1945 and the Constitution making body to be convened as soon as possible
options.d: A solution for the constitutional deadlock

questions.subject: Modern History
questions.explanation: Rajagopalachari’s pamphlet ‘The Way Out’ (1942-44) put forward the C.R. Formula, offering Congress–League cooperation and a plebiscite-based partition plan as a way to resolve the constitutional deadlock between the two parties. It was not about creating a War Advisory Council, reshaping the Viceroy’s Executive Council, or scheduling fresh elections; therefore (a), (b) and (c) are incorrect while (d) is correct. ([chronicleindia.in](https://www.chronicleindia.in/online-magazine/archive-csce-october-2024/c-r-formula-1944?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: There are only two known examples of cave paintings of the Gupta period in ancient India. One of these is paintings of Ajanta caves. Where is the other surviving example of Gupta paintings?
questions.subject: Art & Culture
questions.explanation: Apart from Ajanta, the only other surviving set of Gupta-era mural paintings is found in the Bagh caves (Dhar district, Madhya Pradesh), dating to c. 5th century CE. Ellora’s paintings are later (post-Gupta), while Lomas Rishi and Nasik contain either Mauryan or early-historic carvings but no confirmed Gupta paintings.

questions.question: The United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) is an international treaty drawn at

options.a: United Nations’ Conference on the Human Environment, Stockholm, 1972
options.b: UN Conference on Environment and Development, Rio de Janeiro, 1992
options.c: World Summit on Sustainable Development, Johannesburg, 2002

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.explanation: The UN Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) was opened for signature at the UN Conference on Environment and Development (UNCED) held in Rio de Janeiro from 3–14 June 1992 (Earth Summit). It did not originate at the 1972 Stockholm meet, the 2002 Johannesburg summit, or the 2009 Copenhagen COP.

questions.question: Which bacterial strain, developed from natural isolates by genetic manipulations, can be used for treating oil spills ?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Genetically manipulated strains of Pseudomonas (notably Pseudomonas putida developed by Dr. A. M. Chakrabarty) carry multiple hydrocarbon-degrading plasmids and are patented for cleaning up oil spills. Agrobacterium is used in plant engineering, Clostridium in anaerobic fermentation, and Nitrosomonas in nitrification, none being engineered ‘superbugs’ for oil.

questions.question: Which feature of some species of blue-green algae helps promote them as bio-fertilizers ?

options.a: They convert atmospheric methane into ammonia which the crop plants can absorb readily.
options.b: They induce the crop plants to produce the enzymes which help convert atmospheric nitrogen to nitrates.
options.c: They have the mechanism to convert atmospheric nitrogen into a form that the crop plants can absorb readily.
options.d: They induce the roots of the crop plants to absorb the soil nitrates in larger quantities.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Certain blue-green algae (cyanobacteria such as Anabaena and Nostoc) possess heterocysts that fix atmospheric N₂ into ammonia, supplying usable nitrogen to crops like rice. They do not act on methane, induce plant enzymes, or merely enhance nitrate uptake; direct biological nitrogen fixation is the key feature.

questions.question: India is a party to the Ramsar Convention and has declared many areas as Ramsar Sites. Which of the following statements best describes how we should maintain these sites in the context of this Convention?

options.a: Keep all the sites completely inaccessible to man so that they will not be exploited.
options.b: Conserve all the sites through ecosystem approach and permit tourism and recreation only.
options.c: Conserve all the sites through ecosystem approach for a period without any exploitation, with specific criteria and specific period for each site, and then allow sustainable use of them by future generations.
options.d: Conserve all the sites through ecosystem approach and allow their simultaneous sustainable use.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.explanation: Under Article 3 and subsequent ‘wise use’ guidelines, Parties must conserve the ecological character of all wetlands through an ecosystem approach while permitting their simultaneous sustainable use. The Convention does not demand total exclusion of humans (a), restrict sites only to tourism (b), or mandate a fixed moratorium before use (c).

questions.question:
Other than Jatropha curcas, why is Pongamia pinnata also considered a good option for the production of bio-diesel in India?
1. Pongamia pinnata grows naturally in most of the arid regions of India.
2. The seeds of Pongamia pinnata are rich in lipid content of which nearly half is oleic acid.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.explanation: Pongamia pinnata is a hardy, drought-tolerant leguminous tree that grows naturally across much of dry-land/sem-arid India, making it suitable for marginal arid zones. Its seed oil typically contains ~47–60 % oleic acid, giving a high lipid/mono-unsaturated profile ideal for biodiesel production([researchgate.net](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/225255623_Development_of_Pongamia_pinnata_as_an_alternative_biofuel_crop_-_current_status_and_scope_of_plantations_in_India?utm_source=openai)).

questions.question:
Due to their extensive rice cultivation, some regions may be contributing to global warming. To what possible reason/reasons is this attributable?
1. The anaerobic conditions associated with rice cultivation cause the emission of methane.
2. When nitrogen based fertilizers are used, nitrous oxide is emitted from the cultivated soil.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.explanation: Continuously flooded rice paddies create anaerobic soil conditions that favour methanogenic bacteria, so rice cultivation is a major anthropogenic source of CH₄. In contrast, nitrous-oxide (N₂O) peaks arise mainly from aerobic nitrification/denitrification after heavy N-fertilizer use in upland soils; under the strictly anaerobic conditions of flooded paddy this pathway is limited, so large N₂O emissions are not characteristic. Hence only statement 1 is correct([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010)).

questions.question: Sustainable development is described as the development that meets the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs. In this perspective, inherently the concept of sustainable development is intertwined with which of the following concepts ?
questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.explanation: The Brundtland definition links sustainability to using natural resources at rates that do not exceed the environment’s ‘carrying capacity’—the maximum level an ecosystem can support without degradation. Social justice, inclusive growth or globalisation are relevant but not inherent to the definitional core, whereas carrying capacity is explicitly intertwined with the concept of sustainable development([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010)).

questions.question: Given below are the names of four energy crops. Which one of them can be cultivated for ethanol ?
questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.explanation: Ethanol is commercially produced from starch-rich grain crops; in India and worldwide, maize (corn) is the principal feedstock because its kernels are ~60 % starch that can be hydrolysed and fermented. Jatropha, Pongamia and sunflower are oil-seed crops suited to biodiesel, not ethanol. Therefore maize is the correct energy crop for ethanol cultivation([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010)).

questions.question:
Consider the following pairs:
Protected area — Well-known for
1. Bhitarkanika, Orissa — Salt Water Crocodile
2. Desert National Park, Rajasthan — Great Indian Bustard
3. Eravikulam, Kerala — Hoolak Gibbon
Which of the pairs given above is/are correctly matched ?
questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.explanation: Bhitarkanika National Park in Odisha is famous for its sizeable population of salt-water crocodiles, and Desert National Park in Rajasthan is a stronghold of the critically endangered Great Indian Bustard, so pairs 1 and 2 are correctly matched. Eravikulam National Park, however, is noted for the Nilgiri tahr—not the Hoolock gibbon (which occurs in Northeast India). Hence only pairs 1 and 2 are correct([v1.wii.gov.in](https://v1.wii.gov.in/nmcg/priority-species/reptiles/salt-water-crocodile?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Some species of plants are insectivorous. Why ?

options.a: Their growth in shady and dark places does not allow them to undertake sufficient photosynthesis and thus they depend on insects for nutrition.
options.b: They are adapted to grow in nitrogen deficient soils and thus depend on insects for sufficient nitrogenous nutrition.
options.c: They cannot synthesize certain vitamins themselves and depend on the insects digested by them.
options.d: They have remained in that particular stage of evolution as living fossils, a link between autotrophs and heterotrophs.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.explanation: Carnivorous plants typically grow in bogs and other nitrogen-poor soils; by digesting insects they obtain the nitrogen compounds that the soil cannot supply, enabling normal protein synthesis and growth. Low light or vitamin needs are not the cause, and they are not ‘living fossils’. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Venus_flytrap?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: A person travelled a distance of 50 km in 8 hours. He covered a part of the distance on foot at the rate of 4 km per hour and a part on a bicycle at the rate of 10 km per hour. How much distance did he travel on foot ?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Let the walking distance be x km.  Time taken = x/4 + (50 − x)/10 hours = 8. Solving gives 5x+100–2x = 160 ⇒ 3x = 60 ⇒ x = 20 km.  Hence he walked 20 km and cycled the remaining 30 km.

questions.question:
In the context of governance, consider the following :
1. Encouraging Foreign Direct Investment inflows
2. Privatization of higher educational Institutions
3. Down-sizing of bureaucracy
4. Selling/off-loading the shares of Public Sector Undertakings
Which of the above can be used as measures to control the fiscal deficit in India ?
questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: Reducing expenditure (privatising higher-education institutions, downsizing bureaucracy) and raising non-tax capital receipts (selling/disinvesting PSU shares) directly improve the government’s fiscal balance.  Encouraging FDI affects private capital flows, not the Union government’s budget, so it does not lower the fiscal deficit. ([rbi.org.in](https://www.rbi.org.in/scripts/BS_ViewBulletin.aspx?Id=21781&utm_source=openai))

questions.question: As per the UN-Habitat's Global Report on Human Settlements 2009, which one among the following regions has shown the fastest growth rate of urbanization in the last three decades?
questions.subject: Geography
questions.explanation: UN-Habitat’s Global Report on Human Settlements 2009 notes that Asia has been undergoing the most rapid demographic shift toward cities, with urban population growth far outpacing that of Europe, North America, or Latin America-Caribbean over the previous three decades. ([press.un.org](https://press.un.org/en/2008/081027_habitat.doc.htm?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: In India, which type of forest among the following occupies the largest area?
questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.explanation: Among Champion-&-Seth forest classes, Tropical Moist Deciduous Forests cover the greatest extent (about 38 % of India’s total forest area), stretching across the wetter flanks of the Deccan Plateau, eastern India and the lower Himalayan slopes. Other formations such as montane temperate, dry evergreen or wet evergreen occupy much smaller areas. ([sawaal.com](https://www.sawaal.com/indian-geography-question-and-answers/in-india-which-type-of-forest-among-the-following-occupies-the-largest-area-_10331?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Inclusive growth as enunciated in the Eleventh Five Year Plan does NOT include one of the following:
questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: The Eleventh Plan’s idea of inclusive growth aimed at poverty reduction, expanding employment and narrowing social (including gender) gaps; it did not list strengthening the capital market as an element, hence option (c) is excluded. ([testbook.com](https://testbook.com/question-answer/inclusive-growth-as-enunciated-in-the-eleventh-fiv--6080345676d4833dffae06ff?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: How many numbers from 0 to 999 are NOT divisible by either 5 or 7?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: From 0–999 there are 1,000 numbers. Multiples of 5 = 1,000 ÷ 5 = 200; multiples of 7 = ⌊999/7⌋+1 = 143; multiples of both (LCM 35) = ⌊999/35⌋+1 = 29. Using inclusion-exclusion, numbers divisible by 5 or 7 = 200+143–29 = 314; therefore not divisible by either = 1,000–314 = 686 → option (c). ([rishiupsc.com](https://rishiupsc.com/upsc-prelims-question-paper-2010-with-answers/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
Tamil Nadu is a leading producer of mill-made cotton yarn in the country. What could be the reason?
1. Black cotton soil is the predominant type of soil in the State.
2. Rich pool of skilled labour is available.
Which of the above is/are the correct reasons?
questions.subject: Geography
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu’s dominance in mill-made cotton yarn is attributed to an abundant pool of skilled (and relatively cheap) labour and supportive power supply. Black cotton (regur) soil is not predominant in the state; it is more common in Maharashtra, Gujarat and MP, so statement 1 is wrong and only statement 2 is correct. ([testbook.com](https://testbook.com/question-answer/tamil-nadu-is-the-leading-producer-of-mill-made-co--608039a31ae8d5f99969f6ad?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:
1. On the planet Earth, the fresh water available for use amounts to about less than 1% of the total water found.
2. Of the total fresh water found on the planet Earth 95% is bound up in polar ice caps and glaciers.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Geography
questions.explanation: Usable fresh water (surface water + accessible groundwater) is well under 1 % of all Earth’s water, so statement 1 is correct. Of total fresh water, about 69 % is locked in ice caps/glaciers—not 95 %—making statement 2 incorrect; hence only statement 1 is correct. ([edurev.in](https://edurev.in/t/203174/Physical-Geography-Solved-Questions-2021-1995-1?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Which one of the following reflects back more sunlight as compared to the other three?
questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.explanation: Fresh, clean snow has the highest albedo (≈ 80-90 %), reflecting far more incoming solar radiation than sand deserts, croplands or prairies, so “land covered with fresh snow” reflects back the most sunlight. ([testbook.com](https://testbook.com/question-answer/which-one-of-the-following-reflects-back-more-sunl--60803a421cce4719d77ee29d?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Rivers that pass through Himachal Pradesh are

options.d: Beas, Chenab, Ravi, Satluj and Yamuna

questions.subject: Geography
questions.explanation: All five rivers—Beas, Chenab, Ravi, Satluj and even the Yamuna, which forms part of the southern boundary in Sirmaur district—flow through Himachal Pradesh. Options (a), (b) and (c) omit at least one of these rivers, so only option (d) lists the complete set. ([testbook.com](https://testbook.com/question-answer/rivers-that-pass-through-himachal-pradesh-are--60803a6f907e7564c1c39427?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Who of the following shall cause every recommendation made by the Finance Commission to be laid before each House of Parliament?
questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.explanation: Article 281 of the Constitution states that ‘The President shall cause every recommendation made by the Finance Commission … to be laid before each House of Parliament.’ Hence the constitutional duty rests with the President, not with the Speaker, Prime Minister or Finance Minister. ([indiankanoon.org](https://indiankanoon.org/doc/1945997/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Which one of the following is responsible for the preparation and presentation of Union Budget to the Parliament?
questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: Within the Ministry of Finance, the Budget Division of the Department of Economic Affairs is explicitly responsible for preparing and submitting the Union Budget to Parliament; the other departments deal mainly with revenue collection, financial services or expenditure control. ([dea.gov.in](https://dea.gov.in/about-us?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: In a group of five persons A, B, C, D and E, there is a professor, a doctor and a lawyer. A and D are unmarried ladies, and do not work. Of the married couple in the group, E is the husband. B is the brother of A and is neither a doctor nor a lawyer. Who is the professor?

options.d: Cannot be determined with the available data

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: A and D are non-working unmarried ladies, so the three professions (professor, doctor, lawyer) must be held by B, C and E. B is expressly ‘neither a doctor nor a lawyer,’ so he must be the professor. Options (b) and (c) assign the professor to C or A contrary to the statements, and the data are sufficient—hence option (d) is wrong. ([studocu.com](https://www.studocu.com/in/document/karnataka-state-law-university/llb-3-years/prelims-general-studies-2010-answer-key/27145276?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
Consider the following actions by the Government:
1. Cutting the tax rates
2. Increasing the government spending
3. Abolishing the subsidies
In the context of economic recession, which of the above actions can be considered a part of the "fiscal stimulus" package?
questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: A fiscal-stimulus package during recession uses expansionary tools such as tax cuts (to raise disposable income) and higher government spending (to directly boost demand). Abolishing subsidies would withdraw support and is contractionary, so only statements 1 and 2 qualify. ([investopedia.com](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/s/stimulus-package.asp?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Half of the villagers of a certain village have their own houses. One-fifth of the villagers cultivate paddy. One-third of the villagers are literate. Four-fifth of the villagers are below twenty-five. Then, which one of the following is certainly true?

options.a: All the villagers who have their own houses are literate
options.b: Some villagers under twenty-five are literate
options.c: A quarter of the villagers who have their own houses cultivate paddy
options.d: Half of the villagers who cultivate paddy are literate

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Below-25 villagers = 4⁄5 of the population and literate villagers = 1⁄3. 4⁄5 + 1⁄3 = 17⁄15 > 1, so by the principle of inclusion-exclusion at least (17⁄15 − 1) = 2⁄15 of the villagers must belong to both groups; therefore some villagers under twenty-five are literate. The other statements depend on unknown overlaps and are not necessarily true. ([brilliant.org](https://brilliant.org/wiki/principle-of-inclusion-and-exclusion-pie/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: When the Reserve Bank of India announces an increase of the Cash Reserve Ratio, what does it mean?

options.a: The commercial banks will have less money to lend
options.b: The Reserve Bank of India will have less money to lend
options.c: The Union Government will have less money to lend
options.d: The commercial banks will have more money to lend

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: When the RBI raises the CRR, banks must keep a larger fraction of their deposits as cash with the RBI. This cuts the amount of deposits they can deploy, so commercial banks have less money available for lending to customers. ([economictimes.indiatimes.com](https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/wealth/save/crr-vs-slr-why-rbi-instructs-banks-to-maintain-them-and-how-they-benefit-customers/articleshow/125936804.cms?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Who among the following Governor Generals created the Covenanted Civil Service of India which later came to be known as the Indian Civil Service?
questions.subject: Modern History
questions.explanation: Governor-General Lord Cornwallis (1786-1793) instituted the Covenanted Civil Service through the Cornwallis Code, laying the foundation of what later became the Indian Civil Service. Warren Hastings preceded him, while Wellesley and William Bentinck came later and only modified the system. ([cambridge.org](https://www.cambridge.org/core/journals/journal-of-asian-studies/article/problem-of-recruitment-for-the-indian-civil-service-during-the-late-nineteenth-century/472276E88291F3399AF26BFBD9EF7A60?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: What was the immediate cause for the launch of the Swadeshi movement?

options.a: The partition of Bengal done by Lord Curzon
options.b: A sentence of 18 months rigorous imprisonment imposed on Lokmanya Tilak
options.c: The arrest and deportation of Lala Lajpat Rai and Ajit Singh; and passing of the Punjab Colonization Bill
options.d: Death sentence pronounced on the Chapekar brothers

questions.subject: Modern History
questions.explanation: The Swadeshi (and boycott) movement was triggered immediately by Lord Curzon’s announcement and implementation of the partition of Bengal on 16 October 1905. The other events (Tilak’s imprisonment in 1908, Lajpat Rai’s deportation in 1907, Chapekar brothers in 1899) occurred later or were unrelated to the launch. ([britannica.com](https://www.britannica.com/topic/Swadeshi-Movement?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:
1. Dr. Rajendra Prasad persuaded Mahatma Gandhi to come to Champaran to investigate the problem of peasants.
2. Acharya J. B. Kripalani was one of Mahatma Gandhi's colleagues in his Champaran investigation.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Modern History
questions.explanation: Raj Kumar Shukla, an indigo peasant, persuaded Mahatma Gandhi to visit Champaran, not Dr. Rajendra Prasad, so Statement 1 is incorrect. Acharya J. B. Kripalani was indeed one of Gandhi’s colleagues who helped during the Champaran enquiry, making Statement 2 correct; hence only statement 2 is true. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Raj_Kumar_Shukla?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: By a regulation in 1793, the District Collector was deprived of his judicial powers and made the collecting agent only. What was the reason for such regulation?

options.a: Lord Cornwallis felt that the District Collector's efficiency of revenue collection would enormously increase without the burden of other work
options.b: Lord Cornwallis felt that Judicial power should compulsorily be in the hands of Europeans while Indians can be given the job of revenue collection in the districts
options.c: Lord Cornwallis was alarmed at the extent of power concentrated in the District Collector and felt that such absolute power was undesirable in one person
options.d: The judicial work demanded a deep knowledge of India and a good training in law and Lord Cornwallis felt that District Collector should be only a revenue collector

questions.subject: Modern History
questions.explanation: In the Cornwallis Code (Regulation III of 1793), Lord Cornwallis separated revenue and judicial functions because he feared that conferring both on the Collector created an unhealthy concentration of authority. He therefore removed the Collector’s magisterial/civil powers and placed them in the hands of a separate District Judge, retaining the Collector solely as ‘collector’. Hence option (c) reflects the motive; the other options either mis-state Cornwallis’s rationale or add conditions he never cited. ([zigyan.com](https://www.zigyan.com/ask-questions/by-a-regulation-in-1793-the-district-collector-was-deprived-of-his-judicial?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
With reference to India, consider the following statements:
1. The Wholesale Price Index (WPI) in India is available on a monthly basis only.
2. As compared to Consumer Price Index for Industrial Workers (CPI(IW)), the WPI gives less weight to food articles.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: 1. After October 2009 headline WPI began to be released only on a monthly basis; weekly dissemination was confined to sub-groups and was formally discontinued in 2012, so Statement 1 is treated as correct. 2. Food items carry about 24 % weight in WPI but roughly 46 % in CPI-IW, so Statement 2 is correct. Therefore both statements are correct (option c). ([business-standard.com](https://www.business-standard.com/article/economy-policy/govt-discontinues-weekly-wpi-release-112020200142_1.html))

questions.question: Each person's performance compared with all other persons is to be done to rank them subjectively. How many comparisons are needed in total, if there are 11 persons?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: To compare every person with every other person once, the number of comparisons equals the combination nC2 = n(n − 1)/2. For 11 persons: 11 × 10 / 2 = 55 comparisons ⇒ option (b) 55. ([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: What is the principle by which a cooling system (Radiator) in a motor car works?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: A car radiator rejects engine heat through forced convection: hot coolant circulates through thin tubes while air is driven across the fins, removing heat by convective transfer. Conduction and pure radiation play only minor roles, so option (b) Convection is correct. ([edukemy.com](https://edukemy.com/blog/upsc-prelims-topic-wise-questions-physics-heat-and-temperature/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Which among the following do/does not belong/belongs to the GSM family of wireless technologies?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: EDGE (2.75 G) and LTE (4 G) are both evolutionary standards developed by 3GPP within the GSM family. DSL (Digital Subscriber Line) is a wired broadband technology unrelated to GSM. Thus the option that does NOT belong to the GSM family is DSL – option (c). ([testbook.com](https://testbook.com/question-answer/which-among-the-following-dodoes-not-belongbelon--608044d7983949673096fa43?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: With reference to the treatment of cancerous tumours, a tool called cyberknife has been making the news. In this context, which one of the following statements is not correct?

options.a: It is a robotic image guided system
options.b: It delivers an extremely precise dose of radiation
options.c: It has the capability of achieving sub-millimetre accuracy
options.d: It can map the spread of tumour in the body

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: The Cyberknife is a fully robotic, image-guided stereotactic radiosurgery system that delivers very high-dose beams with sub-millimetre precision; it does not perform diagnostic whole-body tumour mapping, so statement (d) is incorrect while (a)–(c) are correct. ([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010))

questions.question: When you travel in certain parts of India, you will notice red soil. What is the main reason for this colour?
questions.subject: Geography
questions.explanation: Red soils owe their characteristic red colour chiefly to coatings of ferric (iron) oxides on soil particles; magnesium, humus or phosphates are not responsible for the hue. ([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010))

questions.question: Which one of the following is the appropriate reason for considering the Gondwana rocks as most important of rock systems of India?

options.a: More than 90% of limestone reserves of India are found in them
options.b: More than 90% of India's coal reserves are found in them
options.c: More than 90% of fertile black cotton soils are spread over them
options.d: None of the reasons given above is appropriate in this context

questions.subject: Geography
questions.explanation: About 98-99 % of India’s proven coal reserves occur in the Gondwana rock formations, making coal the dominant resource associated with this rock system; limestone and black soils are not so predominantly linked. ([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010))

questions.question: Which one of the following can one come across if one travels through the Strait of Malacca?
questions.subject: Geography
questions.explanation: The Strait of Malacca separates the Malay Peninsula from Sumatra and opens near the major port-city state of Singapore at its southern end; travellers through the strait thus encounter Singapore, not Bali, Brunei or Java. ([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010))

questions.question: With reference to the river Luni, which one of the following statements is correct?

options.a: It flows into Gulf of Khambhat
options.b: It flows into Gulf of Kuchchh
options.c: It flows into Pakistan and merges with a tributary of Indus
options.d: It is lost in the marshy land of the Rann of Kuchchh

questions.subject: Geography
questions.explanation: Originating in the Aravallis, the Luni flows southwest but, being an inland, saline river, finally dissipates into the marshy terrain of the Rann of Kachchh without reaching the sea; the other options describe outlets it does not reach. ([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010))

questions.question: Which one of the following pairs is not correctly matched?  Dam/Lake — River
questions.subject: Geography
questions.explanation: Govind Sagar Reservoir is formed by Bhakra Dam on the Satluj; Ukai Reservoir is on the Tapi; and Wular Lake lies on the Jhelum. Kolleru Lake, however, is a freshwater depression situated BETWEEN the Krishna and Godavari deltas and is fed by local streams and canals, not directly by the Krishna River—hence the pair ‘Kolleru Lake : Krishna’ is wrongly matched. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kolleru_Lake?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
A geographic region has the following distinct characteristics:
1. Warm and dry climate
2. Mild and wet winter
3. Evergreen oak trees
The above features are the distinct characteristics of which one of the following regions?
questions.subject: Geography
questions.explanation: A Mediterranean (Cs) climate is typified by hot or warm, dry summers; mild, wet winters; and sclerophyllous evergreen vegetation such as evergreen oaks. The given set of features matches this climatic region found around the Mediterranean Basin and similar west-coast mid-latitude zones. ([britannica.com](https://www.britannica.com/science/Mediterranean-climate?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
With reference to the National Rehabilitation and Resettlement Policy, 2007, consider the following statements:
1. This policy is applicable only to the persons affected by the acquisition of land for projects and not to the involuntary displacement due to any other reason.
2. This policy has been formulated by the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: the National Rehabilitation & Resettlement Policy (NRRP) 2007 covers ALL cases of involuntary displacement, not just those arising from land acquisition. Statement 2 is incorrect because the policy was issued by the Department of Land Resources, Ministry of Rural Development—not the Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment. Hence neither statement is correct. ([testbook.com](https://testbook.com/question-answer/with-reference-to-the-national-rehabilitation--60804ba482c8ea750e847a7b))

questions.question: In the context of India’s Five Year Plans, a shift in the pattern of industrialization, with lower emphasis on heavy industries and more on infrastructure begins in
questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: The Sixth Five-Year Plan (1980-85) was the first to recognise excess capacity in basic/heavy industries and initiated a strategic shift toward strengthening infrastructure and supportive services; this re-orientation was further carried forward in the Seventh Plan. Therefore the shift ‘begins’ with the Sixth Plan. ([scribd.com](https://www.scribd.com/presentation/722899896/Planning-Market-and-the-State))

questions.question:
Two of the schemes launched by the Government of India for Women’s development are Swadhar and Swayam Siddha. As regards the difference between them, consider the following statements:
1. Swayam Siddha is meant for those in difficult circumstances such as women survivors of natural disasters or terrorism, women prisoners released from jails, mentally challenged women etc., whereas Swadhar is meant for holistic empowerment of women through Self Help Groups.
2. Swayam Siddha is implemented through Local Self Government bodies or reputed Voluntary Organizations whereas Swadhar is implemented through the ICDS units set up in the states.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.explanation: Swadhar (1995) provides shelter and support to women in difficult circumstances—widows, released prisoners, disaster victims, etc.—while Swayam Siddha (2001) is a holistic women-empowerment project based on forming Self-Help Groups. Statement 1 reverses the purposes; Statement 2 misstates the implementing agencies (Swadhar works mainly through NGOs under MWCD, not ICDS units; Swayam Siddha is routed through State agencies/NGOs, not necessarily local bodies). Thus both statements are wrong. ([timesofindia.indiatimes.com](https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/chandigarh/haryana-launches-swadhar/articleshow/14199530.cms?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
With reference to the United Nations Convention on the Rights of the Child, consider the following:
1. The Right to Development
2. The Right to Expression
3. The Right to Recreation
Which of the above is/are the Rights of the child?
questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.explanation: The UNCRC explicitly recognises (i) the child’s right to survival and development (Article 6), (ii) the right to express views freely and have them heard (Article 12), and (iii) the right to rest, leisure, play and recreational activities (Article 31). Hence all three listed rights are included.([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Convention_on_the_Rights_of_the_Child?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Three men start together to travel the same way around a circular track of 11 km. Their speeds are 4, 5.5 and 8 kmph respectively. When will they meet at the starting point for the first time?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Time per lap is 11 km ÷ 4 kmph = 2.75 h, 11 km ÷ 5.5 kmph = 2 h and 11 km ÷ 8 kmph = 1.375 h. The first simultaneous return to the start occurs after the LCM of these times: LCM(2.75, 2, 1.375) = 22 hours, so they meet after 22 hours.([testbook.com](https://testbook.com/question-answer/three-men-start-together-to-travel-the-same-way-ar--5aacb123fbc5741d903dbcdd?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: With reference to the Consumer Disputes Redressal at district level in India, which one of the following statements is not correct?

options.a: A State Government can establish more than one District Forum in a district if it deems fit
options.b: One of the members of the District Forum shall be a woman
options.c: The District Forum entertains the complaints where the value of goods or services does not exceed rupees fifty lakhs
options.d: A complaint in relation to any goods sold or any service provided may be filed with a District Forum by the State Government as a representative of the interests of the consumers in general

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.explanation: Under Section 11 of the Consumer Protection Act 1986 (as it stood in 2010), a District Forum could entertain complaints where the value of goods/services did not exceed ₹20 lakh, not ₹50 lakh. Therefore statement 3 (option c) is incorrect, while the other statements accurately reflect provisions allowing multiple forums, mandatory inclusion of a woman member, and locus standi of State Government.([studyadda.com](https://www.studyadda.com/question-bank/judiciary_q98/4460/346932))
