questions.question: King Cobra is the only snake that makes its own nest. Why does it make its nest?

options.a: It is a snake-eater and the nest helps attract other snakes
options.b: It is a viviparous snake and needs a nest to give birth to its offspring
options.c: It is an oviparous snake and lays its eggs in the nest and guards the nest until they are hatched
options.d: It is a large, cold blooded animal, and needs a nest to hibernate in the cold season

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.explanation: The female King Cobra (Ophiophagus hannah) is the only snake that builds a nest; it is oviparous and constructs a leaf-litter mound, lays its eggs inside and remains to guard them until they hatch. This matches option (c); the other options misdescribe its reproductive mode or purpose.([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/King_cobra))

questions.question: As a result of their annual survey, the National Geographic Society and an international polling firm GlobeScan gave India top rank in Greendex 2009 score. What is this score?

options.a: It is a measure of efforts made by different countries in adopting technologies for reducing carbon footprint
options.b: It is a measure of environmentally sustainable consumer behaviour in different countries
options.c: It is an assessment of programmes/schemes undertaken by different countries for improving the conservation of natural resources
options.d: It is an index showing the volume of carbon credits sold by different countries

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.explanation: The Greendex, produced by the National Geographic Society and GlobeScan, is a composite index that ‘measures consumer behaviour in areas such as housing, transportation, food and goods’ to gauge how environmentally sustainable consumers in different countries are. Thus it is a measure of environmentally sustainable consumer behaviour, making option (b) correct.([globescan.com](https://globescan.com/2014/09/26/increased-fears-about-environment-but-little-change-in-consumer-behavior-national-geographic-globescan-study/))

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:
1. The Taxus tree naturally found in the Himalayas.
2. The Taxus tree is listed in the Red Data Book.
3. A drug called “taxol” is obtained from Taxus tree is effective against Parkinson’s disease.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.explanation: Taxus wallichiana (Himalayan yew) grows naturally in the Himalayan region and is classified as ‘Endangered’, hence recorded in the Red Data Book. Paclitaxel/Taxol obtained from the tree is an anti-cancer chemotherapeutic; it is not used for Parkinson’s disease, so statement 3 is false. Therefore only statements 1 and 2 are correct. ([testbook.com](https://testbook.com/question-answer/consider-the-following-statements-1-the-taxus--608050578fc7b40e1a00304b?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: P, Q, R and S are four men. P is the oldest but not the poorest. R is the richest but not the oldest. Q is older than S but not than P or R. P is richer than Q but not than S. The four men can be ordered (descending) in respect of age and richness, respectively, as
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: From the clues, age order is P > R > Q > S (PRQS) because P is the oldest, R is not oldest, and Q is older than S but younger than P and R. For wealth, R > S > P > Q (RSPQ) since R is richest, S is richer than P, and P richer than Q. Option (b) gives exactly PRQS for age and RSPQ for richness. ([licchavilyceum.com](https://licchavilyceum.com/upsc-2010-prelims-question-paper/))

questions.question: What causes wind to deflect toward left in the Southern hemisphere?
questions.subject: Geography
questions.explanation: In the Southern Hemisphere moving air masses are deflected to the left by the Coriolis force, which arises solely due to Earth’s rotation. Temperature and pressure create winds, but the sideways deflection is a consequence of rotation, not magnetic field. ([licchavilyceum.com](https://licchavilyceum.com/upsc-2010-prelims-question-paper/))

questions.question: Indiscriminate disposal of used fluorescent electric lamps causes mercury pollution in the environment. Why is mercury used in the manufacture of these lamps?

options.a: A mercury coating on the inside of the lamp makes the light bright white
options.b: When the lamp is switched on, the mercury in the lamp causes the emission of ultra-violet radiations
options.c: When the lamp is switched on, it is the mercury which converts the ultra-violet energy into visible light
options.d: None of the statement given above is correct about the use of mercury in the manufacture of fluorescent lamps

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Inside a fluorescent lamp an electric discharge passes through low-pressure mercury vapour; excited mercury atoms emit predominantly ultraviolet radiation. The UV then strikes the phosphor coating, which fluoresces to visible light. Thus mercury’s role is to produce UV, not to coat the glass or directly convert UV to visible photons. ([zeromercury.org](https://www.zeromercury.org/about-mercury/mercury-in-products/lamps/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
If there were no Himalayan ranges, what would have been the most likely geographical impact on India?
1. Much of the country would experience the cold waves from Siberia.
2. Indo-Gangetic plain would be devoid of such extensive alluvial soils.
3. The pattern of monsoon would be different from what it is at present.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Geography
questions.explanation: Without the Himalayan barrier, cold Siberian air would sweep deep into India (statement 1). The great alluvial thickness of the Indo-Gangetic plain is derived from Himalayan erosion; absent the range, such deposits would be minimal (statement 2). The mountains also force and deflect monsoon winds, so their absence would drastically alter the monsoon pattern (statement 3). All three statements are therefore correct. ([licchavilyceum.com](https://licchavilyceum.com/upsc-2010-prelims-question-paper/))

questions.question: In the context of space technology, what is “Bhuvan”, recently in the news?

options.a: A mini satellite launched by ISRO for promoting the distance education in India
options.b: The name given to the next Moon Impact Probe, for Chandrayaan-II
options.c: A geoportal of ISRO with 3-D imaging capabilities of India
options.d: A space telescope developed by India

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: “Bhuvan” is ISRO’s web-based geo-portal that provides high-resolution, 3-D visualisation of the Indian terrain, similar to Google Earth but focused on India. It is not a satellite, probe, or telescope. ([rishiupsc.com](https://rishiupsc.com/upsc-prelims-question-paper-2010-with-answers/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: The latitudes that pass through Sikkim also pass through
questions.subject: Geography
questions.explanation: Sikkim lies roughly between 27° N and 28° N. Of the states listed, only Rajasthan spans this latitude band (≈23° N–30° N). Punjab, Himachal Pradesh and the erstwhile Jammu & Kashmir all lie north of 29 °N, so their territory never reaches Sikkim’s latitude. ([rishiupsc.com](https://rishiupsc.com/upsc-prelims-question-paper-2010-with-answers/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: A man fills a basket with eggs in such a way that the number of eggs added on each successive day is the same as the number already present in the basket. This way the basket gets completely filled in 24 days. After how many days was the basket 1⁄4th full?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: The basket’s contents double each day (adding as many eggs as already present). Working backwards, it was half-full on day 23 and one-quarter full on day 22 (two doublings earlier), so 22 days are required. ([rishiupsc.com](https://rishiupsc.com/upsc-prelims-question-paper-2010-with-answers/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: The diameters of two circular coins are in the ratio of 1 : 3. The smaller coin is made to roll around the bigger coin till it returns to the position from where the process of rolling started. How many times did the smaller coin roll around the bigger coin?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Most coaching-house answer keys for UPSC‐2010 give 3 as the official answer: the ratio of circumferences (big : small = 3 : 1) is treated as the number of complete rotations made by the small coin while its centre moves once around the larger coin. Hence the UPSC key accepts option (c) 3, even though a strict kinematic derivation would predict 4.

questions.question: The difference between the simple interest received from two banks on Rs. 500 for two years is Rs. 2·50. What is the difference between their rates?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Difference in interest = P × ΔR × T / 100 ⇒ 2.5 = 500 × ΔR × 2 / 100 ⇒ ΔR = 0.25 percentage point. Thus the two banks’ annual simple-interest rates differ by 0.25 %. ([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: When ten persons shake hands with one another, in how many ways is it possible?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: The total number of distinct handshakes among n persons is nC2. For 10 persons, 10C2 = (10×9)/2 = 45, so option (d) is correct; the other values underestimate the combination count. ([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010))

questions.question: A candidate attempted 12 questions and secured full marks in all of them. If he obtained 60% marks in the test and all questions carried equal marks, then what is the total number of questions in the test?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: If all questions carry equal marks, 12 correct answers give 12m marks. Scoring 60 % overall means 12m = 0.6 Tm ⇒ T = 12/0.6 = 20 questions. Hence option (d) is correct; the other options correspond to incorrect division of percentages. ([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010))

questions.question: In how many ways can four children be made to stand in a line such that two of them, A and B, are always together?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Keep A and B together as a single unit; along with the other two children there are 3! = 6 ways to arrange the units, and A–B can switch places in 2 ways, giving 6×2 = 12 arrangements. Option (b) is therefore correct; 6 ignores internal swapping, while 18 and 24 over-count. ([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010))

questions.question: In a meeting, the map of a village was placed in such a manner that south-east becomes north, north-east becomes west and so on. What will south become?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: To make original south-east point to north, the map is rotated 135° clockwise. After the same rotation, the original south (180°) shifts to 45°, i.e., north-east. Hence option (b) is correct; north-west or west would arise from incorrect rotation direction. ([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010))

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:
1. The “Bombay Manifesto” signed in 1936 openly opposed the preaching of socialist ideals.
2. It evoked support from a large section of business community from all across India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.subject: Modern History
questions.explanation: The 1936 ‘Bombay Manifesto’, signed by 21 prominent industrialists, expressly denounced Jawaharlal Nehru’s advocacy of socialist ideas (statement 1). It was welcomed by a wider spectrum of Indian big-business groups fearful of state-socialism, so statement 2 is also correct. Therefore option (c) ‘Both 1 and 2’ is right; options (a)/(b) overlook one statement, while (d) rejects both. ([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010))

questions.question: Among the following, who was not a proponent of bhakti cult ?
questions.subject: Art & Culture
questions.explanation: Tukaram, Tyagaraja and Vallabhacharya were well-known Bhakti saints who preached personal devotion to a chosen deity, whereas Nagarjuna was a 2nd-century Mahayana Buddhist philosopher (Madhyamika school) and not connected with the Bhakti cult. Hence only Nagarjuna does not belong to the Bhakti tradition. ([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: For the Karachi session of Indian National Congress in 1931 presided over by Sardar Patel, who drafted the Resolution on Fundamental Rights and Economic Programme ?
questions.subject: Modern History
questions.explanation: At the special Karachi session (26-31 March 1931) chaired by Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, the historic ‘Resolution on Fundamental Rights and Economic Programme’ was drafted by Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru; it laid down civil liberties and socio-economic goals that later influenced the Constitution. ([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Who among the following were official Congress negotiators with Cripps Mission ?

options.b: Acharya J. B. Kripalani and C. Rajagopalachari
options.d: Dr. Rajendra Prasad and Rafi Ahmed Kidwai

questions.subject: Modern History
questions.explanation: When Sir Stafford Cripps visited India in March 1942, the Indian National Congress appointed Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru and Maulana Abul Kalam Azad as its official negotiators; they held the formal talks that ultimately failed. Other pairs in the options did not serve in this official capacity. ([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Which one of the following processes in the bodies of living organisms is a digestive process ?

options.a: Breakdown of proteins into amino acids
options.b: Breakdown of glucose into CO₂ and H₂O
options.d: Conversion of amino acids into proteins

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Digestion is the enzymatic breakdown of complex food molecules into simpler absorbable units; the classic example is proteolysis, where dietary proteins are hydrolysed by pepsin, trypsin, etc., into amino acids. The other processes listed relate to respiration (oxidation of glucose) or assimilation/storage (glycogenesis, protein synthesis). ([testbook.com](https://testbook.com/question-answer/which-one-of-the-following-processes-in-the-bodies--608058f8df024496630adb35?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: From the point of view of evolution of living organisms, which one of the following is the correct sequence of evolution ?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Vertebrate evolution proceeded from fishes to reptiles to mammals. Sharks (cartilaginous fishes) appeared >400 million years ago, reptiles like tortoises evolved later in the Paleozoic–Mesozoic era, and mammals such as otters are the most recent; therefore the correct chronological order is Shark → Tortoise → Otter. ([testbook.com](https://testbook.com/question-answer/from-the-point-of-view-of-evolution-of-living-orga--6080593a6ea787775420a00b?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:
1. Hepatitis B is several times more infectious than HIV/AIDS
2. Hepatitis B can cause liver cancer
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: HBV is a blood-borne virus that is about 100 times more infectious than HIV, and chronic HBV infection is a well-known cause of hepatocellular carcinoma (primary liver cancer). Therefore both statements are correct. ([cdc.gov](https://www.cdc.gov/mmwr/preview/mmwrhtml/rr5201a1.htm?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Excessive release of the pollutant carbon monoxide (CO) into the air may produce a condition in which oxygen supply in the human body decreases. What causes this condition ?

options.a: When inhaled into the human body, CO is converted into CO₂
options.b: The inhaled CO has much higher affinity for haemoglobin as compared to oxygen
options.c: The inhaled CO destroys the chemical structure of haemoglobin
options.d: The inhaled CO adversely affects the respiratory centre in the brain

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Carbon monoxide binds to haemoglobin with roughly 200–300 times the affinity of oxygen, forming carboxyhaemoglobin and blocking oxygen transport; this high affinity—not conversion to CO₂ or neural effects—is what produces hypoxia. ([ncbi.nlm.nih.gov](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK539815/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:
1. Every individual in the population is equally susceptible host for Swine Flu.
2. Antibiotics have no role in the primary treatment of Swine Flu.
3. To prevent the future spread of Swine Flu in the epidemic area, the swine (pigs) must all be culled.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: All age groups can be infected by pandemic H1N1 (statement 1) and antibiotics do not treat viral influenza; antivirals are used instead (statement 2). International animal-health bodies (FAO/OIE/WHO) stated that mass culling of pigs was unjustified, so statement 3 is incorrect. Hence only 1 and 2 are correct. ([cdc.gov](https://www.cdc.gov/flu/treatment/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: With regard to the transmission of the Human Immunodeficiency Virus, which one of the following statements is not correct ?

options.a: The chances of transmission from female to male are twice as likely as from male to female
options.b: The chances of transmission are more if a person suffers from other sexually transmitted infections
options.c: An infected mother can transmit the infection to her baby during pregnancy, at childbirth and by breast feeding
options.d: The risk of contracting infection from transfusion of infected blood is much higher than an exposure to contaminated needle

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Epidemiological studies show male-to-female transmission of HIV is nearly twice as efficient as female-to-male, so statement (a) is the inaccurate one, while the other statements correctly describe factors that raise transmission risk. ([pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/1392708/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
What are the possible limitations of India in mitigating the global warming at present and in the immediate future ?
1. Appropriate alternate technologies are not sufficiently available.
2. India cannot invest huge funds in research and development.
3. Many developed countries have already set up their polluting industries in India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.explanation: Limited availability of cost-effective alternative technologies (1) and the high upfront R&D investment India would require (2) are acknowledged constraints; the blanket assertion that many developed countries have already shifted their polluting industries to India is not broadly valid, so only 1 and 2 hold true. ([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010))

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:
1. The Commonwealth has no charter, treaty or constitution.
2. All the territories/countries once under the British empire (jurisdiction/rule/mandate) automatically joined the Commonwealth as its members.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.explanation: In 2010 the Commonwealth operated only on political declarations (e.g., 1949 London, 1971 Singapore, 1991 Harare); a formal, single Charter was adopted later in 2013, so it had no charter/treaty/constitution at that time, making statement 1 correct. Membership is voluntary—many former British territories never joined (e.g., USA) or withdrew—hence statement 2 is wrong. Therefore only statement 1 is correct. ([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010))

questions.question:
Examine the following statements :
1. All colours are pleasant.
2. Some colours are pleasant.
3. No colour is pleasant.
4. Some colours are not pleasant.
Given that the statement 4 is true, what can be definitely concluded ?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: If statement 4 ('Some colours are not pleasant') is true, then the universal statement 1 ('All colours are pleasant') cannot be true—so 1 is definitely false. Statements 2 and 3 may or may not hold, but at least we can conclude that statement 1 is false; option (b) expresses this. ([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010))

questions.question:
In the context of bilateral trade negotiations between India and European Union, what is the difference between European Commission and European Council ?
1. European Commission represents the EU in trade negotiations whereas European Council participates in the legislation of matters pertaining to economic policies of the European Union.
2. European Commission comprises the Heads of State or government of member countries whereas the European Council comprises of the persons nominated by European Parliament.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.explanation: The European Commission conducts external trade negotiations for the EU (e.g., at the WTO), while the European Council (ministers in Council configurations) co-legislates on EU economic laws together with the European Parliament. Hence statement 1 is correct. Statement 2 reverses the compositions: the Commission is made up of Commissioners (not heads of state), whereas the European Council brings together the heads of state/government; so statement 2 is incorrect. ([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010))

questions.question: The approximate representation of land-use classification in India is

options.a: Net area sown 25%; forests 33%; other areas 42%
options.b: Net area sown 58%; forests 17%; other areas 25%
options.c: Net area sown 43%; forests 29%; other areas 28%
options.d: Net area sown 47%; forests 23%; other areas 30%

questions.subject: Geography
questions.explanation: Standard land-use statistics (circa 2008-09 used by UPSC) place roughly 47 % of India’s geographical area under net sown area, 23 % under forests, and the remaining 30 % under all other categories. Only option (d) matches this approximate distribution; the other options mis-state one or more percentages. ([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010))

questions.question:
With reference to the National Investment Fund to which the disinvestment proceeds are routed, consider the following statements :
1. The assets in the National Investment Fund are managed by the Union Ministry of Finance.
2. The National Investment Fund is to be maintained within the Consolidated Fund of India.
3. Certain Asset Management Companies are appointed as the fund managers.
4. A certain proportion of annual income is used for financing select social sectors.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: NIF corpus raised from disinvestment is managed by three public-sector Asset Management Companies—SBI, LIC and UTI—appointed as fund managers, and 75 % of the annual income (at the time of the question) was earmarked for education, health and other social schemes; so statements 3 and 4 are correct. The assets are managed by these AMCs, not directly by the Finance Ministry, and the Fund is kept outside the Consolidated Fund of India (it is maintained in the Public Account), so statements 1 and 2 are incorrect. Hence only 3 and 4 are correct. ([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010))

questions.question: In India, which of the following is regulated by the Forward Markets Commission ?

options.d: Both Commodities Futures and Financial Futures Trading

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: Before its 2015 merger with SEBI, the Forward Markets Commission (FMC) was the statutory regulator for commodity futures/forward contracts; equity and currency derivatives were regulated by SEBI and RBI respectively, so only commodities futures trading fell under the FMC’s purview. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Forward_Markets_Commission?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Which one of the following is not a feature of Limited Liability Partnership firm ?

options.b: Partnership and management need not be separate
options.c: Internal governance may be decided by mutual agreement among partners
options.d: It is corporate body with perpetual succession

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: The LLP Act, 2008 treats an LLP as a body corporate with perpetual succession, allows partners to frame internal governance by mutual agreement, and does not require separation of ownership and management. Crucially, it prescribes no upper limit on the number of partners (unlike an ordinary partnership, which is capped at 20/50). Hence the statement that ‘partners should be less than 20’ is NOT a feature. ([complianceindia.co.in](https://complianceindia.co.in/limited-liability-partnership-llp-want-know/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: With reference to the institution of Banking Ombudsman in India, which one of the statements is not correct ?

options.a: The Banking Ombudsman is appointed by the Reserve Bank of India
options.b: The Banking Ombudsman can consider complaints from Non-Resident Indians having accounts in India
options.c: The orders passed by the Banking Ombudsman are final and binding on the parties concerned
options.d: The service provided by the Banking Ombudsman is free of any fee

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: RBI appoints the Banking Ombudsman and the service is free; NRIs with Indian accounts can file complaints. However, the Ombudsman’s award is appealable to the Deputy Governor of RBI, so it is not ‘final and binding’—making option (c) the incorrect statement. ([thelaw.institute](https://thelaw.institute/business-law-as-applicable-to-co-operative-ii/appeal-banking-ombudsman-decisions/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
With reference to India, consider the following :
1. Nationalization of Banks
2. Formation of Regional Rural Banks
3. Adoption of villages by Bank Branches
Which of the above can be considered as steps taken to achieve the “financial inclusion” in India ?
questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: Bank nationalization (1969 & 1980) vastly expanded rural branching; creation of Regional Rural Banks (1975) targeted credit to small and marginal farmers; and later schemes under which individual bank branches ‘adopt’ villages aim to bring basic banking services to unbanked areas. All three are explicitly intended to further financial inclusion, so options 1, 2 and 3 are relevant. ([bis.org](https://www.bis.org/review/r250611b.htm?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: What was the immediate reason for Ahmad Shah Abdali to invade India and fight the Third Battle of Panipat ?

options.a: He wanted to avenge the expulsion by Marathas of his viceroy Timur Shah from Lahore
options.b: The frustrated governor of Jullundhar Adina Beg Khan invited him to invade Punjab
options.c: He wanted to punish Mughal administration for non-payment of the revenues of the Chahar Mahal (Gujarat, Aurangabad, Sialkot and Pasrur)
options.d: He wanted to annex all the fertile plains of Punjab up to the borders of Delhi to his kingdom

questions.subject: Modern History
questions.explanation: In 1758 the Marathas drove Abdali’s son and viceroy, Timur Shah, out of Lahore; Abdali’s 1759–61 campaign culminating in the Third Battle of Panipat was launched primarily to avenge this humiliation and re-establish Afghan authority in Punjab. Other options refer to earlier or long-term factors but not the immediate trigger. ([sarthaks.com](https://www.sarthaks.com/2714746/what-was-immediate-reason-ahmad-shah-abdali-invade-india-and-fight-the-third-battle-panipat?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
With reference to Pondicherry (now Puducherry), consider the following statements :
1. The first European power to occupy Pondicherry were the Portuguese.
2. The second European power to occupy Pondicherry were the French.
3. The English never occupied Pondicherry.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.subject: Modern History
questions.explanation: Portuguese merchants were the first Europeans to establish a trading centre at Pondicherry in 1521. After them the Dutch (not the French) captured the town in 1693, and the British occupied it several times (1761, 1793, 1803, 1804) before restoring it to France; hence statements 2 and 3 are wrong, leaving only statement 1 correct. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pondicherry))

questions.question:
Why did Buddhism start declining in India in the early medieval times ?
1. Buddha was by that time considered as one of the incarnations of Vishnu and thus became a part of Vaishnavism.
2. The invading tribes from Central Asia till the time of last Gupta king adopted Hinduism and persecuted Buddhists.
3. The Kings of Gupta dynasty were strongly opposed to Buddhism.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.subject: Ancient History
questions.explanation: A major reason for Buddhism’s decline was its assimilation into Hinduism when the Buddha came to be regarded as an avatar of Vishnu (statement 1). There is no clear evidence that Central-Asian tribes who invaded up to the last Gupta king adopted Hinduism and systematically persecuted Buddhists, nor were the Gupta rulers ‘strongly opposed’—some (e.g., Kumaragupta) even patronised Nalanda. Thus statements 2 and 3 are incorrect. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Decline_of_Buddhism_in_the_Indian_subcontinent?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
Consider the following statements :
The functions of commercial banks in India include
1. Purchase and sale of shares and securities on behalf of customers.
2. Acting as executors and trustees of wills.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: Besides accepting deposits and lending, Indian commercial banks provide secondary/agency services such as (i) buying and selling shares or other securities for customers and (ii) acting as trustees or executors of wills and estates. Therefore both statements are correct. ([insurance-companies.co](https://insurance-companies.co/commercial-bank-functions/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: In India, the tax proceeds of which one of the following as a percentage of gross tax revenue has significantly declined in the last five years ?
questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: Between 2005-06 and 2009-10 the share of Union excise duty in the Centre’s gross tax revenue almost halved because of repeated cuts in excise rates and the growing importance of service tax, while the shares of service tax, income tax and corporation tax either rose or stayed broadly stable. Hence excise duty registered the sharpest relative decline. ([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Which one of the following authorities makes recommendation to the Governor of a State as to the principles for determining the taxes and duties which may be appropriated by the Panchayats in that particular State ?

options.d: Panchayati Raj Ministry of that State

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.explanation: Under Article 243-I, the State Finance Commission is constituted every five years "to make recommendations to the Governor as to the principles which should govern … the taxes and duties which may be assigned to, or appropriated by, the Panchayats." Therefore the State Finance Commission (not district planning bodies or state ministries) advises the Governor on this matter. ([constitutionofindia.net](https://www.constitutionofindia.net/articles/article-243i-constitution-of-finance-commission-to-review-financial-position/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
Consider the following statements :
In India, taxes on transactions in Stock Exchanges and Futures Markets are
1. levied by the Union
2. collected by the States
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: Entry 90 of the Union List empowers Parliament to levy and collect taxes on the sale-or-purchase of securities; this is implemented through the Securities Transaction Tax (STT) that is both levied and collected by the Union government, so statement 1 is correct.  States have no role in collecting this tax, so statement 2 is wrong. ([testbook.com](https://testbook.com/question-answer/consider-the-following-statements-in-india-tax--608063c23a3dce5296b4c391?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: In India, during the last decade the total cultivated land for which one of the following crops has remained more or less stagnant ?
questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: Official answer keys show that, unlike rice, oilseeds or sugarcane, the gross sown area under pulses hovered around 21-23 million ha throughout 2000-2009 and therefore remained largely stagnant; hence option (c) Pulses is correct. ([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010))

questions.question:
Consider the following statements :
1. The Union Government fixes the Statutory Minimum Price of sugarcane for each sugar season.
2. Sugar and sugarcane are essential commodities under the Essential Commodities Act.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: The Central (Union) Government notifies the Statutory Minimum Price/FRP of sugarcane for every sugar season, fulfilling statement 1.  Under the Essential Commodities Act, 1955 both sugar and sugarcane are notified as essential commodities, so statement 2 is also correct; therefore both statements are true. ([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010))

questions.question:
With reference to Indian economy, consider the following statements :
1. The Gross Domestic Product (GDP) has increased by four times in the last 10 years.
2. The percentage share of Public Sector in GDP has declined in the last 10 years.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: Between 1999-00 and 2009-10 India’s GDP at current prices rose roughly 2.5-3 times (from about ₹23 trillion to ₹63 trillion) – nowhere near a four-fold increase – so statement 1 is incorrect.  During the same decade liberalisation and PSU disinvestment reduced the public sector’s share of GDP (from ≈34 % to ≈26 %), making statement 2 correct.  Hence only statement 2 is right. ([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010))

questions.question:
Consider the following which can be found in the ambient atmosphere :
1. Soot
2. Sulphur hexafluoride
3. Water vapour
Which of the above contribute to the warming up of the atmosphere ?
questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.explanation: All three listed constituents warm the atmosphere: (1) soot/black-carbon particles absorb solar radiation and reduce albedo; (2) sulphur hexafluoride is the most potent long-lived greenhouse gas (≈23,500 × CO₂); (3) water vapour is the dominant natural greenhouse gas.  Therefore 1, 2 and 3 all contribute to atmospheric warming. ([iasexamportal.com](https://iasexamportal.com/Answer-Key/General-Studies-Solution-CSP-2010))

questions.question: The International Development Association, a lending agency, is administered by the

options.a: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: The International Development Association (IDA) is the concessional window of the World Bank Group and is legally affiliated with – and administered by – the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (IBRD). Hence option (a) is correct; the agencies in options (b)–(d) have no administrative control over IDA. ([britannica.com](https://www.britannica.com/topic/International-Development-Association?utm_source=openai))
